summary: Tested Light/Dark theme toggle feature. All core functionality working. One minor bug found: inactive nav items show a persistent rgba(255,255,255,0.08) background in dark mode due to CSS specificity issue.

ui_bugs.issue: Inactive nav items show persistent rgba(255,255,255,0.08) background in dark mode. The .wt-sidebar CSS rule `[class*='bg-slate-100']` matches nav elements with `hover:bg-slate-100` class (substring match), applying background persistently even when not hovered.

action_items: Fix: In index.css, change `.wt-sidebar [class*='bg-slate-100']` to be more specific so it doesn't match `hover:bg-slate-100`. One approach: target `.wt-sidebar .bg-slate-100` (exact class match) instead of the substring [class*='bg-slate-100'] selector. Or add a specific override: `.wt-sidebar nav a:not([style*='background']) { background-color: transparent !important; }`
critical_code_review_comments: index.css: `.wt-sidebar [class*='bg-slate-100']` uses substring match and unintentionally matches `hover:bg-slate-100` Tailwind utility class on nav items, causing persistent faint background on inactive items in dark mode sidebar.

test_results.dark_mode_activates: PASS - data-theme set to 'dark' on click
test_results.card_bg_white_overridden: PASS - bg-white elements become rgb(30,41,59) in dark
test_results.inactive_nav_no_bg: FAIL - rgba(255,255,255,0.08) persistent on inactive nav items
test_results.light_restore: PASS - sidebar returns to white, data-theme='default'
test_results.theme_persistence: PASS - dark theme persists after page reload

context_for_next_testing_agent: Theme toggle fully functional. Only minor issue: inactive nav items in dark mode sidebar have rgba(255,255,255,0.08) persistent background due to [class*='bg-slate-100'] matching hover:bg-slate-100. Note: admin user had old 'ocean' theme saved, which defaults to light appearance since no CSS rules exist for it.
rca_of_issue: Root Cause: In index.css, `.wt-sidebar [class*='bg-slate-100']` is an attribute substring selector. Tailwind generates `hover:bg-slate-100` class on nav anchor elements. Since 'bg-slate-100' is a substring of 'hover:bg-slate-100', the CSS rule matches and applies background-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.08) persistently (not just on hover). Fix: use `.wt-sidebar .bg-slate-100` (exact class match) or add a transparent override specifically for nav links.
